business.headline: Mandatory but delayed: Why third-party motor insurance claims take years to settleHandcrafted ByOur Editors In the fourth story in our series Motor Mess, which looks at issues in motor insurance, we spoke to industry experts to understand why claims take years to settle.

Air India to cut international flights till July amid fuel surge, airspace curbsAir India will cut international flights from June to July as rising ATF prices, airspace restrictions, and longer routes make several operations unprofitable, CEO Campbell Wilson said.

How best to tackle oil shock? Dr Sajjid Z Chinoy respondsIn an interview with India Today, Dr Sajjid Z Chinoy, Chief India Economist at JP Morgan, discussed the global energy shock and its impact on the Indian economy following the recent hikes in commercial LPG and anticipated domestic fuel price increases.

Not complying with trade deal: Trump hikes tariffs on cars, trucks from EU to 25%The move comes months after Trump and European Commission President Ursula von der Leyen agreed to a new trade deal in July 2025, which set a 15 per cent tariff on most goods, including automobiles and auto parts.

Petrol, diesel prices may be hiked by Rs 4-5 per litre, LPG by Rs 40-50: SourcesPetrol and diesel prices may increase by Rs four to five per litre, while domestic LPG cylinder prices could rise by Rs 40-50 per cylinder, according to government sources.

Fuel, LPG prices may go up, decision likely within 5-7 days: Government sourcesPetrol, diesel prices may rise by Rs 4–5 per litre and LPG by Rs 40–50 per cylinder, following a sharp jump in global crude oil prices driven by West Asia war.

Will your minimum pension rise from Rs 1,000 to Rs 7,500? Find out who benefitsPensioners across the country may soon see a shift in their monthly income if the proposed changes go through. Here's what we know so far and who could benefit.

8th Pay Commission to visit Hyderabad, Srinagar, Ladakh: What does this mean?The 8th Pay Commission is taking its discussions beyond Delhi, with visits planned to Hyderabad, Srinagar and Ladakh. But what does this outreach mean for government employees and pensioners awaiting revisions?

Paytm sees strong user trust and high merchant stickiness post PPBL actionBrokerage firms tracking the company said the RBI action on PPBL has limited direct impact on Paytm's core business operations, given the structural separation between the listed entity and the payments bank.

Earning Rs 50,000 a month? The right way to spend, save and investManaging money at Rs 50,000 a month isn't just about covering expenses, it's about making choices that shape your financial future. But are you allocating it in the right way?

From petrol to food, why could everything get costlier togetherA mix of higher oil prices, a weaker rupee and monsoon risks is putting pressure on India's economy. The overlap could lift household costs, limit policy choices and raise stagflation concerns.

GST at record Rs 2.43 lakh crore in April, but domestic demand growth slowsA major part of April's growth came from import-related revenues. GST collected on imports rose sharply by 25.8% to Rs 57,580 crore.

Workplace injury claims jump 31%: Are businesses doing enough?On Labour Day, as conversations around worker safety and rights take centre stage, a pressing question emerges—are businesses truly doing enough to protect their workforce? A recent report suggests concerns are growing.

Even the bank didn't help me: What victims face after reporting digital fraudFor part two of our series on Banking Risks in Digital India, I spoke to victims of digital fraud to understand what they experienced after being scammed. Their stories show how the aftermath can be just as distressing as the fraud itself.

Commercial LPG up by nearly Rs 1,000: Why have prices increased?Commercial LPG cylinder prices have risen again as global oil tensions keep costs elevated. The move shields households for now but raises pressure on restaurants, hotels and small businesses.

Maharashtra Day on May 1: Is the stock market open or closed today?The BSE and NSE are closed on May 1 for Maharashtra Day across key trading segments. Commodity derivatives will reopen in the evening, while traders should account for settlement and liquidity effects.

Commercial, mini LPG prices hiked by Rs 993 and Rs 261, no change in domestic ratesCommercial LPG cylinder prices have surged by Rs 993 to Rs 3,071.50 in Delhi, while domestic LPG, petrol, diesel, and kerosene rates remain unchanged to shield consumers.

Bajaj Finance raises FD rates up to 7.40%: Should you lock in now?The company has increased rates by up to 45 basis points, with the biggest jump seen in longer tenures. This means investors looking to park money for a few years could now earn better returns.

Markets end lower as crude stays high, rupee hits record lowMarkets closed lower on Thursday as high crude prices and a weak rupee weighed on sentiment. The slide highlighted persistent inflation and capital flow concerns, even as IT and pharma offered limited support.

Bajaj Finserv announces dividend, reports strong FY26 growthBajaj Finserv reported record FY26 income and profit and announced a special dividend with its Q4 results. The update also marked the completion of Allianz's stake exit, leaving its insurance businesses fully Bajaj-owned.

entertainment.headline: Lyricist-writer Prasoon Joshi appointed Prasar Bharati Chairman Prasoon Joshi has been appointed Chairman of Prasar Bharati. The lyricist-writer brings experience from films, advertising and his role as CBFC (Central Board of Film Certification) chief.

Rishab Shetty meets Amit Shah, praises his understanding of culture, cinema Rishab Shetty described his meeting with Amit Shah as memorable, highlighting their discussion on cinema, culture and heritage. The actor also shared that the Home Minister appreciated Kantara and spoke about his upcoming projects.

Is Tara Sutaria dating Aditya Roy Kapur after Veer Pahariya split rumours? Is Tara Sutaria dating Aditya Roy Kapur? A new report suggests so, even as speculation continues around her reported split with Veer Pahariya.

Pati Patni Aur Woh Do trailer: Ayushmann Khurrana juggles between 3 relationships The trailer of Pati Patni Aur Woh Do shows Ayushmann Khurrana as a man caught between multiple relationships, leading to confusion and comic situations. Also starring Sara Ali Khan, Rakul Preet Singh and Wamiqa Gabbi, the film is set to release on May 15, 2026.

Factcheck: Tabu upset with Akshay, Priyadarshan for edited role in Bhooth Bangla? Bhooth Bangla director Priyadarshan has cleared the air over reports that Tabu was displeased with him and lead actor Akshay Kumar for alleged edits in her role. Read here what he said.

Kiara Advani shares sun-kissed Maldives moments with Sidharth, daughter Saraayah Kiara Advani's Maldives trip with Sidharth Malhotra is all about love, laughter and motherhood, as she shares family moments with daughter Saraayah. From beach time to reading sessions, the actor offers a glimpse into her life as a new mother.

Diljit Dosanjh says I don't fear death at Canada concert, video sparks concern A video from Diljit Dosanjh's Canada concert captured him speaking about death, detachment and Punjab. His remarks drew concern online, as fans discussed trolling, pressure, and mental health.

Oscars 2027 rule change allows multiple International Feature entries per country Oscars 2027 will see major rule changes as the Academy of Motion Picture Arts and Sciences allows multiple acting nominations in the same category and expands international film eligibility.

Swara Bhasker hits back at postpartum trolls: My body isn't your business Swara Bhasker has responded to trolling over her postpartum weight after sharing a throwback post. She said she chose not to "bounce back" and criticised the pressure on women to meet beauty standards after childbirth.

Usha Mangeshkar says she feels lonely at home after sisters Lata and Asha's death Usha Mangeshkar spoke about the shock of losing sisters Lata Mangeshkar and Asha Bhosle. She said the silence at home has deepened her grief and loneliness.

Patriot box office Day 1: Mohanlal-Mammootty film posts strong global debut Patriot opened strongly in theatres, earning Rs 29.37 crore worldwide on its first day. The Mohanlal and Mammootty reunion, backed by solid Kerala and overseas numbers, drove the opening.

Raja Shivaji box office Day 1: Riteish's film records strong Marathi opening Raja Shivaji opened strongly on Maharashtra Day, collecting around Rs 11.35 crore domestically. Led by its Marathi version, the film set a new opening benchmark and drew solid occupancy across the state.

RGV says Riteish Deshmukh looks like Shivaji reincarnated, praises box office run Ram Gopal Varma has praised Riteish Deshmukh's Raja Shivaji after its theatrical release and strong start at the box office. His post highlighted Riteish's work as both director and actor while underscoring the film's early theatrical momentum.

Does Orry shower with Bisleri? His cheeky reply on influencer's reel sparks buzz Orry reacted with a cheeky comment to an influencer's reel claiming that a Bollywood actor bathes only in Bisleri water on set. His response sparked online speculation, amused reactions, and curiosity about the actor.

KJo goes gaga over The Devil Wears Prada 2: This sequel speaks of our time Karan Johar shared an Instagram note after watching The Devil Wears Prada 2 in theatres. He said the sequel revives nostalgia while reflecting anxieties around journalism, print and corporate power.

Lights, camera, vlog! Why Bollywood and TV celebs are turning YouTubers lately YouTube has emerged as a popular platform for many celebrities in the showbiz world to garner fans and big money. What began as a lockdown experiment for many has now evolved into a powerful space to connect and tell stories, through vlogs and podcasts.
Munawar Faruqui, wife Mehzabeen welcome first child Munawar Faruqui and Mehzabeen Faruqui have welcomed their first child together. The couple got married in May 2024.

Producer Firoz Nadiadwala files fraud case over Hera Pheri rights, claims extortion Film producer Firoz Nadiadwala has filed a fraud complaint in Mumbai over the copyright and remake rights of Hera Pheri. He has alleged an attempt to usurp his rights, extort money and disrupt his company's public listing.

Gladiators: Ajith Kumar's racing film first glimpse out on 55th birthday Ajith Kumar's racing film Gladiators – In Pursuit of Challenges unveiled its first glimpse on his 55th birthday. It brings his training and team to theatres while spotlighting his international motorsport campaign.

general news.headline: Repolling in Bengal's Falta on May 21, EC flags subversion of democratic processIn an official statement, the Election Commission said that repolling will take place in all 285 booths across Falta Assembly Consitituency on May 24. The move came after the BJP alleged large-scale EVM tampering in the area.

Delhi judge found dead at home; family claims he fought with wife hours ago, criedA call to police, a locked bathroom, and a rushed hospital visit - what unfolded inside a Delhi home has left many questions unanswered as authorities begin probing the circumstances behind a judge's death.

Thomas Cup semi-final live: Prannoy in must-win situation after Kidambi losesIndia vs France Live, Thomas Cup semi-final badminton: Ayush Shetty and Kidambi Srikanth have lost their matches against France. India trail 0-2 in the semi-finals, and now they have to win this next match. HS Prannoy comes out to play the third singles match. He has to win to keep India alive in this tie.

Heavy rain, thunderstorm in several parts of Delhi-NCR, alert issuedSudden hail and rain lash Delhi-NCR after days of extreme heat, triggering weather alerts. Thunderstorms and gusty winds disrupt conditions as meteorologists warn of continued instability and patchy but intense rainfall activity.

Isha Ambani's power move at Met Gala pre-party: A dress that maps IndiaIsha Ambani arrived at Vogue Met Gala pre-party in New York City wearing a special dress that featured 26 distinct borders representing different regions of India.

Deaths in Jabalpur cruise boat tragedy rise to 11, bodies of 2 more children foundThe death toll in the Bargi Dam cruise boat tragedy in Jabalpur rose to 11 after two boys' bodies were recovered on Saturday. The accident has intensified the search for the missing and scrutiny of boating safety and alleged negligence.

The know-all Bengalis are clueless this timeThe argumentative Bengali isn't getting into debates on the outcome of the 2026 West Bengal Assembly election. For the first time in living memory, the Shabjaanta Gamchawala are entirely clueless, and this hints at something.

Laid-off engineer with 18 years of experience now works at McDonald'sA Reddit post claiming an experienced software engineer is now working at McDonald's after being laid off due to AI has gone viral. The story has reignited concerns over how artificial intelligence is change hiring in the tech industry.

Watch: First full Moon of May rises like a flower in stunning sky showA video of May's Flower Moon rising behind Whaleback Light near New Castle has gone viral on X. The clip has renewed interest in the full moon's seasonal name and the rare Blue Moon due later this month.

Nobody heard me: Woman who confronted BJP minister over traffic jam shares accountTeena Choudhry said she confronted Maharashtra minister Girish Mahajan after being stuck in a traffic jam and ignored by police for over an hour.

Godrej Industries lays out 2031 growth ambitionsThe purpose statement, 'Crafting tomorrow since 1897', celebrates the Godrej Group's legacy while clearly positioning the new, refreshed Godrej Industries for the future.

Lyricist-writer Prasoon Joshi appointed Prasar Bharati ChairmanPrasoon Joshi has been appointed Chairman of Prasar Bharati. The lyricist-writer brings experience from films, advertising and his role as CBFC (Central Board of Film Certification) chief.

Marta Kostyuk caps off dream Madrid Open title with backflip celebration: WatchMarta Kostyuk clinched her maiden Madrid Open title by defeating Mirra Andreeva 6-3, 7-5, extending her unbeaten clay run to 12 matches. After the win, she celebrated with a stunning backflip, reflecting how much the title meant to her.

'Could not breathe': 6 trapped in Nagpur mall lift smash glass to escapeMinutes turned tense inside a stalled lift, panic rose, and help seemed delayed, until those trapped took matters into their own hands, forcing a dramatic escape that raises serious safety concerns.

Meerut man breaks down after wife found dead at home. Then a twistPolice in Meerut arrested a man accused of killing his wife and staging it as a robbery. The case pointed to repeated domestic strife and leaves their three daughters without either parent.

Protests, repolling, arrests and a Mamata huddle keep Bengal on edge before D-dayRepolling, protests and political manoeuvring kept South 24 Parganas tense on Saturday ahead of counting. Allegations of intimidation and trading of charges sharpened the contest as parties prepared for May 4.

Thrown off a train in January, Kuldeep Kumar breaks pole vault national recordKuldeep Kumar, 21-year-old from Madhya Pradesh cleared 5.41m in Bhubaneswar to break the national pole vault record, months after being stranded at Panvel station for carrying his poles.
Question Time: Weekly News QuizThink you read the headlines this week? Let's test that!

India tests emergency alert SMS that works without SIM card: ExplainedMillions of users across India received a loud government test alert on phones as part of a new disaster warning system. Here is how it works, why some phones got it without a SIM card, and why others may have missed it.

Forget Rs 40 crore retirement fund, Indian students can't even save Rs 3 croreHandcrafted ByOur Editors India's Rs 40 crore retirement estimate by Sandeep Jethwani may be mathematically sound, but salary data tells a different story. With most graduates earning modest incomes and job stability still a concern, reports from PLFS, Unstop, and income surveys reveal a widening gap between what Indians earn and what they need to retire.

Lights, camera, vlog! Why Bollywood and TV celebs are turning YouTubers latelyYouTube has emerged as a popular platform for many celebrities in the showbiz world to garner fans and big money. What began as a lockdown experiment for many has now evolved into a powerful space to connect and tell stories, through vlogs and podcasts.

Shimla cop suspended after viral video shows him kicking taxi driver during spatA viral video from Shimla shows an ASI allegedly kicking a taxi driver during a dispute over parked vehicles amid a VVIP movement. The clip triggered online outrage, leading to the officer's suspension and a departmental inquiry.

Train, post and repeat: A survival guide for India's up-and-coming athletesFor India's unsung athletes, fame is rare, and sponsorships are rarer. In a country like India where sports beyond cricket are rarely rewarded, athletes are finding a new way to survive. India Today spoke to triple jumper Niharika Vashisht on the new realities of athletics in India.

War with US will likely resume: Iran claims Washington not committed to agreementsIran's armed forces said a US-Israeli war on the country was likely to resume. The statement underscored rising tensions and Tehran's claim that Washington is not honouring agreements.

sports.headline: Marta Kostyuk caps off dream Madrid Open title with backflip celebration: Watch Marta Kostyuk clinched her maiden Madrid Open title by defeating Mirra Andreeva 6-3, 7-5, extending her unbeaten clay run to 12 matches. After the win, she celebrated with a stunning backflip, reflecting how much the title meant to her.

PBKS make heartwarming gesture for elderly fan struck by ball in Mullanpur Punjab Kings invited fan Krishan Chand to their Dharamsala match after he was injured by a six in Mullanpur, with Priyansh Arya and Preity Zinta reaching out in a heartfelt gesture.

Babar Azam as captain again? Pakistan star opens up on fresh prospects Babar Azam has kept the door open for a return as Pakistan captain, stating he will do what's best for the team. Under Babar, Peshawar Zalmi have advanced to the PSL final where they will face Marnus Labuschagne's Hyderabad Kingsmen.

BCCI set to review Impact Player rule after end of IPL 2026: Saikia The Indian Cricket Board will be reviewing the Impact Player rule in the Indian Premier League. The rule, which allows teams one substitution per match, has seen a lot of flak from players and experts.

Who is Raghu Sharma? Punjab leg-spinner makes IPL debut for MI in El Clasico IPL 2026, CSK vs MI: Raghu Sharma made his IPL debut for Mumbai Indians against Chennai Super Kings at the MA Chidambaram Stadium, receiving his maiden cap from Hardik Pandya amid MI's struggling season.

Thrown off a train in January, Kuldeep Kumar breaks pole vault national record Kuldeep Kumar, 21-year-old from Madhya Pradesh cleared 5.41m in Bhubaneswar to break the national pole vault record, months after being stranded at Panvel station for carrying his poles.

Thomas Cup semi-final live: Prannoy in must-win situation after Kidambi loses India vs France Live, Thomas Cup semi-final badminton: Ayush Shetty and Kidambi Srikanth have lost their matches against France. India trail 0-2 in the semi-finals, and now they have to win this next match. HS Prannoy comes out to play the third singles match. He has to win to keep India alive in this tie.

Ferguson sees no wrong in Jamieson's wicket celebration of Vaibhav Sooryvanshi IPL 2026: Lockie Ferguson defended Kyle Jamieson after the IPL cautioned him for his send-off to Vaibhav Sooryavanshi. The caution fed a wider debate over on-field aggression, while Ferguson also addressed Punjab Kings' bowling inconsistencies.

Radha, Yastika return in India's strong 15-member squad for Women's T20 World Cup Yastika Bhatia and Radha Yadav have returned as India named a strong 15-member squad for the Women's T20 World Cup and the T20I series against England. Kashvee Gautam and Amanjot Kaur missed out due to injuries. Nandini Sharma earned her maiden India call-up after her impressive showing for DC in WPL 2026.

Will Pathirana play for KKR against SRH? Bowling coach Southee reveals Kolkata Knight Riders are yet to decide whether Matheesha Pathirana will play against Sunrisers Hyderabad. Tim Southee said the call will depend on conditions, overseas balance and matchday selection.

No Lakshya Sen as India face tricky France in semi-finalsThomas Cup 2026: India's top-ranked badminton player Lakshya Sen is out of the semi-finals clash against France. This comes as a huge set-back for the Indian side who would have wanted Lakshya to play the first singles match against a tricky opponent.

Is MS Dhoni back for CSK? Ex-teammate Suresh Raina spills the beans ahead of MI clashIPL 2026: MS Dhoni could return for CSK against MI, with Suresh Raina hinting at a possible comeback after his injury layoff. Coach Michael Hussey confirmed Dhoni is fit to bat and keep, with only his running fitness delaying a return.

Went to India's dressing room: Pak captain Agha recalls Asia Cup handshake rowSalman Ali Agha revealed that the Pakistan team went to meet Indian players and shake hands with them but found the dressing room door shut. India and Pakistan have maintained the no-handshake policy since the 2025 Asia Cup.

Why did Kohli go unpunished? Fans back Jamieson over Sooryavanshi celebrationKyle Jamieson was warned and given a demerit point after his charged send-off to 15-year-old Vaibhav Sooryavanshi in the IPL. The episode reopened questions over how the league polices aggression, celebration and consistency in its conduct rules.

Vaibhav Sooryavanshi at 15 is the big prize and Jamieson's send-off proves itKyle Jamieson dismissed Vaibhav Sooryavanshi with a yorker and gave him a fiery send-off in Jaipur. Rajasthan Royals batting coach Vikram Rathour said the reaction showed how prized the 15-year-old's wicket has become in cricket.

Can India beat giant-slayers France? All you need to know about Thomas Cup semisThomas Cup 2026 semi-finals: India will have their work cut out on Saturday, May 2, as they take on giant-killers France in the semi-final of the Thomas Cup 2026. With a strong singles line-up, France will be up for the test but India will once again bank on Lakshya Sen, Ayush Shetty and Satwik-Chirag to step up and deliver the win.

PSG-Bayern, What a disaster game: Guardiola attends Stockport game, mocks UCL clashPep Guardiola joked about skipping PSG vs Bayern's 9-goal thriller, calling it a "disaster game" as he instead watched Stockport County in League One. He also brushed off fixture congestion talk as Manchester City chase Arsenal in the Premier League title race.

Josh Inglis back for bottom-of-the-table LSG, boosts morale ahead of MI clashIPL 2026: Jos Inglis has reportedly rejoined Lucknow Super Giants before their clash with Mumbai Indians. His return comes as LSG battle poor form with Matthew Breetzke heading home for personal reasons.

Train, post and repeat: A survival guide for India's up-and-coming athletesFor India's unsung athletes, fame is rare, and sponsorships are rarer. In a country like India where sports beyond cricket are rarely rewarded, athletes are finding a new way to survive. India Today spoke to triple jumper Niharika Vashisht on the new realities of athletics in India.

CSK vs MI preview: Hardik Pandya under fire as Mumbai Indians seek Chepauk revengeMumbai Indians travel to Chepauk to face Chennai Super Kings with Hardik Pandya under scrutiny. The match puts two struggling five-time champions under pressure as the season nears an awkward finish.

Sooryavanshi send-off costs Jamieson: DC star penalised for RR game flashpointKyle Jamieson was warned and given a demerit point after an aggressive send-off to Vaibhav Sooryavanshi in Jaipur. The episode triggered criticism online towards Jamieson for the way he celebrated after taking Sooryavanshi's wicket.

KL Rahul 2.0: From strike-rate sceptic to becoming a modern T20 believerKL Rahul said he has worked on his strike-rate after scoring 75 off 40 in Delhi Capitals' win over Rajasthan Royals in Jaipur. His numbers this season, and his comments on modern T20 batting, underline a marked shift in how he approaches the format now after once calling strike-rate overrated.

IPL Play of the Day: Mitchell Starc's big-game aura saw DC cash in on experienceMitchell Starc's well-timed strikes broke RR's momentum at key moments. His experience shaped DC's comeback win in Jaipur.

Mumbai Indians trust Suryakumar Yadav: Coach Jayawardane backs struggling starMahela Jayawardene has backed Suryakumar Yadav despite the batter's lean IPL 2026 run ahead of Mumbai Indians' match against Chennai Super Kings. With Mumbai ninth after two wins in eight matches, the side need a quick turnaround and are relying on senior players to deliver.

Riyan Parag unfazed after vaping row, says he does not have to answer criticsRR vs DC, IPL 2026: Riyan Parag gave a defiant response following his side's defeat to Delhi Capitals in Jaipur. The RR captain believes that he is not answerable to his critics after his recent incident where he was seen vaping in the team dressing room.

Axar Patel dedicates DC win vs RR to fans who died in Delhi road accidentAxar Patel dedicated Delhi Capitals' win over Rajasthan Royals to two fans killed in a road accident after the RCB match. The tribute followed DC's record chase in Jaipur and the captain emphasised on the personal loss felt by the franchise.

Riyan Parag's heroics in vain as Delhi Capitals break the shackles with record chaseIPL 2026, RR vs DC: Delhi Capitals snapped their three-match losing streak on Friday, beating the Rajasthan Royals by seven wickets in Jaipur after recording their highest run-chase in the history of the tournament.

3 T20 World Cup winning captains in 1 frame: Dhoni, Rohit, Suryakumar reuniteIPL 2026: MS Dhoni, Rohit Sharma and Suryakumar Yadav were photographed together during a practice session before the CSK vs MI match at Chepauk. The image drew attention as India's three T20 World Cup-winning captains head into IPL 2026 with injury and form concerns.

Jannik Sinner surpasses Big 3 to create history with Madrid Open final berthJannik Sinner continued his dominant 2026 form by defeating Arthur Fils 6-2, 6-4 to reach the Madrid Open final, becoming the youngest player ever to qualify for the final of all nine ATP Masters 1000 tournaments.

Harshit Rana, Akash Deep expected to miss Afghanistan Test and England tourAkash Deep and Harshit Rana are unlikely to be fit for India's Afghanistan series and UK white-ball tour. Their rehabilitation is progressing, but August's Duleep Trophy looks a more realistic return point.

education.headline: The role of data analytics in modern advertising educationThis transformation also exercises a significant impact on the realm of 'advertising education.'

NCERT deletes Azad Pak, adds China's aggression reference in Class 12 textbookThe NCERT has made final changes to its Class 12 Political Science textbook syllabus for the upcoming academic year, including a revision in reference to India's border situation with China, Azad Pakistan, Article 370 and more.

NEET row: Bihar cops find 6 post-dated cheques 'issued for question paper brokers'Six post-dated cheques which were suspected to have been issued in favour of mafia demanding over Rs 30 lakh from each candidate seeking alleged leaked question paper ahead of NEET held last month were recovered by Bihar Police.

Forest fires surge tenfold from 2004 to 2017: Causes, impact and preventionAmidst a relentless surge, intense forest fires are now multiplying tenfold, casting a menacing shadow over our flora and fauna. Year by year, their fiery grip tightens, threatening ecosystems far and wide, now extending their reach to impact human lives.

Fresh plea in Supreme Court to recall NEET results, reconduct examsA fresh petition has been filed in the Supreme Court to recall the National Entrance cum Eligibility Test - Under Graduate (NEET-UG) 2024 results and re-conduct fresh examination after discrepancies in the declared results.

NTA issues clarification after NEET result controversy over toppers, high cut-offIn a detailed statement, the National Testing Agency (NTA) said that the increase in cut-off and number of toppers in the NEET-UG reflect the competitive nature of the exam and maintained that the test's integrity has not been compromised.

Exclusive: Arvind Kejriwal claims 'BJP succession war, PM backing Amit Shah'Speaking exclusively to India Today TV, Delhi Chief Minister Arvind Kejriwal claimed that there is a "succession war" within the BJP. He also claimed that the ruling party is "not fighting the Lok Sabha elections as a team".

4 medical students arrested in Bihar for appearing as proxies for NEET aspirantsFour MBBS students who appeared in place of NEET aspirants in the medical entrance exam were detained in Bihar's Purnea.

NEET-UG question paper not leaked, claims baseless: National Testing AgencyThe reports claiming question paper leak in the medical entrance exam NEET-UG are "completely baseless and without any ground", the National Testing Agency (NTA) has clarified.
The generation with answers: Tech-savvy Indian youth tackling global challengesIndia's tech-savvy youth rewriting the global narrative, one solution at a time.

Left sweeps JNU students' polls, Dhananjay is first Dalit presidentThe Left swept the Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls, winning all four posts and trouncing the RSS-affiliated ABVP. The ABVP was leading at one point, but the Left eventually surged ahead and emerged victorious.
The power of knowledge: Education's influence on real estate market trendsIn a dynamic sector like real estate, investing in continuing education is not simply an option; it is a requirement.

JNU students' body election on March 22 after 4 yearsThe Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls will take place on March 22, more than four years after the last elections were held in September 2019.

UP 12th board exam math, biology papers leaked on WhatsApp, complaint filedThe mathematics and biology question papers for the Uttar Pradesh class 12 board exam were allegedly leaked on a WhatsApp group.

UP Police Constable recruitment exam cancelled days after paper leakThe Uttar Pradesh Police Constable recruitment exam held on February 17 and 18 was canceled due to a paper leak. The Chief Minister has announced a re-examination within the next six months.
